raw.objective: “to determine whether there is a clinical sign or marker that can predict hepatotoxicity of VPA”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, SEX, WT, DDVPA, comedication (CBZ, PHT, PHB, ZON, CLO) Included: WT, DDVPA, SEX, PHT, CBZ, PHB
raw.observations: Mild elevations in transaminases or ammonia are found to be associated with higher VPA CL and a A/F ratio and a lower TC and FC concentration. A first report of a VPA CL relation with transaminases. N.B.: VPA CL is 2-3 higher in Caucasians than in Japanese
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.equations: CL = (theta1*((WT/40)**theta2)*(DOSE**theta3)*(theta4**(1-SEX))* (theta5**PHT)*(theta6**CBZ)*(theta7**PHB))* exp(eta1) Cipred=DOSE/CL Cobs=Cipred #no error model reported DOSE: daily dose of VPA (mg/day) WT: total body weight (kg) SEX: gender (1:female, 0:male) PHT: comedication phenytoin (1: yes, 0:no) CBZ: comedication carbamazepine (1: yes, 0:no) PHB: comedication phenobarbital (1: yes, 0:no) Tau: dosing interval in hours
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 0.012 theta2= 0.34 theta3= 0.55 theta4= 0.90 theta5=1.32 theta6= 1.11 theta7= 1.12 omega1=0.039601 sigma1= 0.0097

extraction_target.title: Potential relationships between transaminase abnormality and valproic acid clearance or serum carnitine concentrations in Japanese epileptic patients.

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL = (theta1*((WT/40)**theta2)*(DOSE**theta3)*(theta4**(1-SEX))* (theta5**PHT)*(theta6**CBZ)*(theta7**PHB))* exp(eta1) Cipred=DOSE/CL Cobs=Cipred #no error model reported DOSE: daily dose of VPA (mg/day) WT: total body weight (kg) SEX: gender (1:female, 0:male) PHT: comedication phenytoin (1: yes, 0:no) CBZ: comedication carbamazepine (1: yes, 0:no) PHB: comedication phenobarbital (1: yes, 0:no) Tau: dosing interval in hours
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
study.objective: “to determine whether there is a clinical sign or marker that can predict hepatotoxicity of VPA”

covariates.raw: Tested: AGE, SEX, WT, DDVPA, comedication (CBZ, PHT, PHB, ZON, CLO) Included: WT, DDVPA, SEX, PHT, CBZ, PHB

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
